title: Octopoma calycinum
taxonomy: Octopoma calycinum is classified within the family Aizoaceae, which includes succulent plants. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Octopoma belonging to the subfamily Ruschioideae. The genus Octopoma is native to South Africa and is characterized by its succulent leaves and stems. Octopoma calycinum is a specific species within this genus, known for its unique appearance and growth habits. The classification of Octopoma calycinum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other succulent plants in the Aizoaceae family.

intro: Octopoma calycinum, also known as the five-stamened stonecrop, is a succulent plant species belonging to the family Crassulaceae. The health benefits of Octopoma calycinum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Octopoma calycinum include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including acne and eczema.
